seoTitle: How to Connect Microsoft Excel to n8n Workflows
metaDescription: Learn how to link Microsoft Excel to n8n using Azure OAuth credentials so you can append rows and automate spreadsheet updates.
intro: Automating spreadsheet updates with n8n means connecting through Microsoft's Azure platform, since Excel Online authentication runs on OAuth2. This guide covers registering your app, linking credentials in n8n, and mapping data into worksheet rows.

steps.heading: Add the Microsoft Excel node
steps.body: Place the node onto your workflow canvas where you want to interact with your spreadsheet.

steps.heading: Create a credential profile
steps.body: Set up a new n8n credential and copy the OAuth redirect URL it generates.

steps.heading: Register your app in Azure
steps.body: Go to the Microsoft Azure portal to register an application and generate your client credentials.

steps.heading: Paste your keys into n8n
steps.body: Copy your client ID and secret back into n8n to complete the authentication.

steps.heading: Map your worksheet operation
steps.body: Choose to append, update, or read rows, then connect your incoming data to the correct columns and test it.

faq.question: Do I need an Azure account to connect Excel to n8n?
faq.answer: Yes, connecting Excel Online requires registering an application through the Microsoft Azure portal.

faq.question: Can n8n append new rows to an existing spreadsheet?
faq.answer: Yes, the Microsoft Excel node supports appending rows along with other operations like reading and updating.

faq.question: Does this work with local Excel files instead of Excel Online?
faq.answer: This integration is built around the cloud-based Excel Online API rather than local desktop files.

faq.question: What credentials does the Excel node need?
faq.answer: It needs an OAuth2 connection set up through your Azure app registration, including a client ID and secret.
